Fixed in e4d0d43. Root cause: on pane close (TERMINAL_CLOSED/EOF) the client folded the layout + resized its local mirror but never sent a TERMINAL_RESIZE for the survivor's grown dims, so the survivor's server PTY stayed at the split size and the shell kept rendering small. Fix: FrameOutcome.reflow_panes (set by TerminalClosed/TerminalSpawned arms) -> driver diffs folded layout vs pre-frame rects and emits per-leaf TERMINAL_RESIZE (same path as SIGWINCH). Verified: split 80x40 -> survivor stty size 19 80; after exit -> 39 80. Also fixes latent split-side sibling-shrink gap.

Won't-fix (premise doesn't hold): the per-row Vec<u8> bodies prime_reference allocates ARE the consumer's reference grid — N row bodies must be materialized once and retained, so N content allocations are irreducible. reset_geometry's rows_body = vec![Vec::new(); rows] are zero-capacity empties (no heap to reuse), and prime runs ONCE per consumer attach (not per tick). The per-TICK hot path already reuses capacity via mem::swap (synthesize_against_reference). 24 one-time allocs/attach is negligible and irreducible; no real win available.

Deferred (low value for v0.1, needs a wire-stack change to do right): the WS write_frame copy (frame.to_vec() into Message::Binary) is structural under the pinned tokio-tungstenite 0.24, whose Message::Binary owns a Vec<u8>. Avoiding it requires changing FrameWriter::write_frame from &[u8] to an owned Vec<u8>/Bytes so the WS path MOVES into Message::Binary -- a core Transport-trait signature change that only benefits the secondary browser/WS path (the primary UDS path is already zero-copy: write_all(&frame)). The clean version composes with a future tungstenite 0.26 bump (Bytes-based Message), which also lets the already-Bytes TerminalOutput payload flow through with no copy at all. Not worth the trait churn for the WS path pre-1.0.

Fixed in e5f52fc: split synthesize_against_reference into prepare_tick (render grid ONCE per tick into shared tick_* buffers + 1 cursor/mode capture + precomputed epilogue/screen-toggle) and diff_consumer (per-consumer compare+emit). tick_emit renders once for N consumers instead of N times (and 1 mode-FFI batch vs 5N). Wrapper preserves the single-consumer API. Verified: 27/27 state-sync tick tests + full workspace nextest 1400 pass.
